intent: We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent consisting of a Swift/SwiftUI shell (UI, Touch ID auth, Keychain, XPC channel, process management) and a Python 3.12 backend (consensus engine with Claude+GPT-4o parallel generation, build pipeline with confidence-gated scope through operator-gated PR merge, GitHub integration, document store with FAISS vector retrieval, multi-agent coordination ledger, holistic code review, TRD development workflow with FOUNDER/ENGINEER/CONSULTANT modes, self-healing observability loop, cross-run learning via BuildMemory and BuildRules, and Mac CI runner infrastructure). The system is fully specified across 16 TRDs plus supporting documents, with five identified gaps (TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK, deployment pipeline, Figma MCP integration, EmailNotifier contract, and WebhookReceiver details) to be resolved during PRD decomposition as the operator has directed.

Clarifications:
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices
  - resolve as part of the PRD process with best practices

Operator on 'The 16 TRDs specify a large system — should the PRD decompos':
build the PRDs in the order that allows for maximum code efficiency

Operator on 'TRD-17 (Self-Healing) references runtime telemetry and healt':
included in the build

Operator on 'TRD-7 v2.0 CONSULTANT mode (§21) includes client summary .do':
included in the build

Operator on 'TRD-8 v3.0 Health Dashboard and Figma pipeline UI are signif':
include them in the build

prd_plan.title: Platform Foundations, Canonical Contracts, and Gap Resolution
Security Architecture and Safety Enforcement Layer
Backend Runtime, Startup Handshake, XPC Server, and Command Routing
Recovery, Thread State, Checkpointing, and Cross-Run Learning
Document Store, Parsing, Embeddings, Retrieval, and Context Safety
GitHub Integration Core, Webhooks, CI Polling, and Repository Bootstrap
Consensus Engine and Provider Orchestration
Build Pipeline Orchestrator, Scope Gate, PR Planning, and Execution Routing
Code Quality Engine: Self-Correction, Lint Gate, and Fix Loop
Multi-Agent Coordination Ledger and Collaboration Surfaces
Holistic Code Review Workflow and Fix PR Automation
TRD Development Workflow with FOUNDER, ENGINEER, and CONSULTANT Modes
Swift Shell Core: App Structure, Auth, Keychain, and Process Management
Swift-Python XPC Bridge and Shell-Backend Event Integration
SwiftUI Product UX: Build Stream, TRD UX, Health Dashboard, and Remediation Views
Document Import, Sketch Input, Figma Integration, and Figma MCP Pipeline
Crafted Connect SDK, Target Registry, Runtime Telemetry, and In-App Health Reporting
Self-Healing Observability, Detection, Diagnosis, Remediation, and Learning Loop
Notifications, EmailNotifier, Operator Alerts, and Native macOS Attention Surfaces
Mac CI Runner, Swift Validation Pipeline, Signing, Notarization, and Release Deployment
Comprehensive Testing, Regression Enforcement, and Release Validation

1.title: Repository layout, ownership map, and foundational directory structure
1.summary: Create the canonical repository directory structure: forge-standards/, forge-contracts/ (with xpc/, api/, events/, fixtures/ subdirs), forge-models/ (with swift/, python/ subdirs), docs/decisions/ (with gaps/ subdir). Includes CODEOWNERS mapping each directory to its owning team/role, README.md files explaining each directory's purpose, forge-standards/REPO_LAYOUT.md as the source-of-truth document describing the full tree and ownership semantics, and forge-standards/CHANGE_CONTROL.md defining the process for modifying frozen contracts.

2.title: Cross-language naming conventions and serialization standards
2.summary: Define and freeze forge-standards/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md covering: Swift camelCase ↔ Python snake_case mapping rules, JSON wire-format field naming (snake_case canonical), enum serialization (lowercase_with_underscores), date/time (ISO 8601 with timezone), UUID format, semantic versioning format, and file naming conventions. Also defines forge-standards/SERIALIZATION.md specifying JSON as canonical wire format, msgpack as optional binary format, encoding (UTF-8), max message sizes, and null vs absent field semantics.

3.title: Cross-TRD reconciliation decision log
3.summary: Produce the authoritative docs/decisions/DECISIONS.md recording every cross-TRD conflict resolution per FR-1.3: XPC message type naming (TRD-1 vs TRD-12 vs TRD-4), ready message schema (TRD-1 vs TRD-12), credential delivery timing (TRD-1 vs TRD-12), PR entry schema fields (TRD-4 vs TRD-3), health issue severity levels, telemetry event naming, BuildMemory storage format, ReviewIssue categorization, and all other identified inconsistencies. Each entry follows DEC-NNN format with date, conflicting TRD references, conflict description, resolution, rationale, and authoritative TRD designation. Includes docs/decisions/DECISION_TEMPLATE.md for future entries.

4.title: Semantic versioning, compatibility contracts, and operational acceptance limits
4.summary: Define forge-standards/VERSIONING.md covering XPC protocol version negotiation, min/max supported version ranges, backward-compatible vs breaking change definitions, version bumping policy, Swift↔Python version compatibility matrix, and deprecation timeline. Define forge-standards/COMPATIBILITY.md specifying acceptance contracts: unknown message handling (ignore with warning log), unknown JSON fields (preserve and forward), rate limits (per-message-type defaults), size limits (XPC payload max 16MB, individual field limits), retry behavior (exponential backoff with jitter, max 3 retries), timeout defaults, and fail-closed defaults for all security-relevant paths. Also includes forge-standards/OPERATIONAL_LIMITS.md with concrete numeric thresholds for rate limits, timeouts, backoff parameters, and idempotency rules.

5.title: Gap resolution documents for TRD-18, deployment pipeline, Figma MCP, EmailNotifier, and WebhookReceiver
5.summary: Resolve all five identified specification gaps as formal documents under docs/decisions/gaps/. GAP-001: TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK — public API surface, event model, authentication flow, webhook registration contract. GAP-002: Deployment pipeline — CI/CD stages, artifact signing, notarization flow, update channel semantics. GAP-003: Figma MCP integration — MCP server interface, design token extraction contract, asset pipeline. GAP-004: EmailNotifier — notification interface, template model, delivery guarantees, retry policy. GAP-005: WebhookReceiver — endpoint schema, signature verification, event routing, replay protection.

6.title: Canonical JSON schemas for XPC message types
6.summary: Define all canonical JSON Schema (Draft 2020-12) files for XPC message types under forge-contracts/xpc/. Derived from reconciled TRD-1/TRD-12/TRD-4 decisions (PR-3) and naming conventions (PR-2). Includes: message_envelope.schema.json, ready.schema.json, credentials.schema.json, build_card.schema.json, build_status.schema.json, build_result.schema.json, pr_created.schema.json, review_request.schema.json, review_result.schema.json, health_check.schema.json, health_status.schema.json, telemetry_event.schema.json, error.schema.json, shutdown.schema.json, heartbeat.schema.json, capabilities.schema.json, and common.schema.json for shared $defs. Each schema includes $id, title, description, required fields, typed constraints, and examples. MESSAGE_CATALOG.md lists all types, directions, and trigger conditions.

7.title: Canonical JSON schemas for domain models
7.summary: Define JSON Schema files for shared domain entities under forge-contracts/schemas/domain/. Includes: prd.schema.json, pr_spec.schema.json, thread_state.schema.json, build_memory.schema.json, build_rules.schema.json, health_issue.schema.json, review_issue.schema.json, telemetry_event.schema.json, target_registry.schema.json, and common.schema.json for shared domain $defs (enums, value objects). These are the canonical contracts for entities referenced by multiple TRDs and both language stacks.

8.title: Canonical JSON schemas for API contracts and event types
8.summary: Define JSON Schema files for API-level contracts (forge-contracts/api/) and internal event types (forge-contracts/events/). API schemas: github_pr.schema.json, github_review.schema.json, github_webhook_payload.schema.json, consensus_request.schema.json, consensus_response.schema.json, figma_mcp_request.schema.json, figma_mcp_response.schema.json, email_notification.schema.json, webhook_inbound.schema.json, crafted_connect_event.schema.json. Event schemas: build_lifecycle.schema.json, agent_coordination.schema.json, learning_event.schema.json, health_alert.schema.json. Derived from gap resolutions (PR-5) and reconciliation decisions (PR-3).

9.title: Schema validation test harness and domain schema tests
9.summary: Python-based test harness that validates all JSON schemas are well-formed Draft 2020-12, validates golden fixtures against their schemas, and rejects deliberately malformed payloads. Includes forge-contracts/validate_schemas.py CI script, requirements-validation.txt, valid and invalid fixtures for all domain schemas, and tests covering required field enforcement, type constraints, enum values, and pattern constraints.

10.title: XPC and API schema validation tests with security-critical rejection tests
10.summary: Extends the schema validation harness with tests and fixtures for XPC transport schemas and API/event schemas. Includes tests for: unknown message type in envelope (must parse without crash), oversized payload markers, missing required security fields (credentials, signature), handshake ordering expectations, and invalid webhook payloads. Security-critical because these tests validate the XPC attack surface.

11.title: Python canonical domain models — core types, enums, and base mixins
11.summary: Implement Python 3.12 canonical domain model foundations as Pydantic v2 models under forge-models/python/forge_models/. Covers: enums (BuildStatus, Severity, ConsensusStrategy, AgentMode, ReviewCategory, HealthIssueType, TelemetryLevel), base mixins (TimestampMixin, VersionedMixin, SerializableMixin with to_json/from_json/to_dict), and core value objects (SemanticVersion, TargetRef, FileRef, CostRecord). Includes pyproject.toml with Pydantic v2 dependency. All field names follow snake_case per NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md.

12.title: Python canonical domain models — PRD, PRSpec, and ThreadState
12.summary: Implement Pydantic v2 models for PRD (sections, acceptance criteria, dependency references), PRSpec (file_changes, test_requirements, consensus metadata, estimated_complexity), and ThreadState (message history, token counts, cost accumulation, agent mode context). References enums and base types from PR-11.

13.title: Python canonical domain models — BuildMemory, BuildRules, and learning types
13.summary: Implement Pydantic v2 models for BuildMemory (per-project historical build outcomes, failure patterns, timing statistics, cost history with rolling averages), BuildRules (learned heuristics: file-pattern → likely-failure mappings, confidence-threshold overrides, project-specific rules, test-ordering preferences), and supporting types LearningEvent and BuildOutcome. BuildMemory includes serialization to/from document store format. BuildRules includes validation for rule conflict detection.

14.title: Python canonical domain models — HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, and TelemetryEvent
14.summary: Implement Pydantic v2 models for HealthIssue (severity, issue_type, affected_component, description, suggested_remediation, auto_fixable, first_seen/last_seen), ReviewIssue (category, file_path, line_range, description, suggestion, confidence_score, blocking), and TelemetryEvent (event_name, level, timestamp, component, payload, correlation_id, session_id). Models validate against corresponding JSON schemas from PR-7.

15.title: Python canonical domain models — TargetRegistry and XPC message models
15.summary: Implement Pydantic v2 models for TargetRegistry (target_id, platform, build_system, source_paths, dependency_graph, build_settings) and all XPC message models corresponding to PR-6 schemas: ReadyMessage, CredentialsMessage, BuildCardMessage, BuildStatusMessage, BuildResultMessage, PRCreatedMessage, ReviewRequestMessage, ReviewResultMessage, HealthCheckMessage, HealthStatusMessage, TelemetryEventMessage, ErrorMessage, ShutdownMessage, HeartbeatMessage, and MessageEnvelope with type discriminator. Each model includes from_xpc_payload(dict) and to_xpc_payload() methods.

16.title: Python forge-models package finalization — exports, py.typed, and metadata
16.summary: Finalize the forge-models Python package: update __init__.py with __all__ exporting all public models, add py.typed marker for PEP 561, add version.py with __version__ following semantic versioning contract, add comprehensive docstrings referencing authoritative schemas and TRDs, update pyproject.toml with complete metadata (classifiers, URLs, Python >=3.12). Includes smoke test importing every public symbol.

17.title: Swift canonical domain models — core types, enums, and protocols
17.summary: Implement Swift canonical domain model foundations under forge-models/swift/Sources/ForgeModels/. Covers: enums (BuildStatus, Severity, ConsensusStrategy, AgentMode, ReviewCategory, HealthIssueType, TelemetryLevel) as Codable enums with snake_case raw values, protocols (Timestamped, Versioned, ForgeSerializable with toJSON/fromJSON), value objects (SemanticVersion, TargetRef, FileRef, CostRecord), and Package.swift for the ForgeModels Swift package. All CodingKeys match canonical snake_case JSON format.

18.title: Swift canonical domain models — XPC message types and envelope
18.summary: Implement Swift Codable structs for all XPC message types matching PR-6 schemas: ReadyMessage, CredentialsMessage, BuildCardMessage, BuildStatusMessage, BuildResultMessage, PRCreatedMessage, ReviewRequestMessage, ReviewResultMessage, HealthCheckMessage, HealthStatusMessage, TelemetryEventMessage, ErrorMessage, ShutdownMessage, HeartbeatMessage, and MessageEnvelope with type-discriminated decoding. Includes XPCMessageType enum with string raw values. Each struct conforms to ForgeSerializable.

19.title: Swift canonical domain models — HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent, and TargetRegistry
19.summary: Implement Swift Codable structs for HealthIssue, ReviewIssue, TelemetryEvent, and TargetRegistry matching Python counterparts and JSON schemas. These are the domain models the Swift UI shell needs for rendering health dashboards, review results, telemetry displays, and target selection. Each conforms to ForgeSerializable and Equatable.

20.title: Swift ForgeModels package finalization — public API surface and module exports
20.summary: Finalize the ForgeModels Swift package: add ForgeModels.swift barrel file re-exporting all public types, verify all types have proper access control (public), add DocC documentation comments referencing authoritative schemas and TRDs, update Package.swift with platform requirements (.macOS(.v14)), and add PackageExportTests.swift validating all expected types are accessible.

21.title: Cross-language serialization round-trip tests with golden fixtures
21.summary: Comprehensive test suite validating every canonical domain model serializes identically between Swift and Python. Golden JSON fixtures (one per model type) stored in forge-contracts/fixtures/. Python tests deserialize each fixture and re-serialize, asserting semantic equivalence. Swift tests do the same. Tests validate: unknown field preservation (extra fields survive round-trip), null vs absent field handling, date format consistency, enum case consistency, MessageEnvelope type discrimination, and camelCase/snake_case mapping correctness. This is the integration gate — if these tests pass, Swift and Python can communicate without schema drift.

22.title: Acceptance contract behavioral tests — unknown messages, size limits, retry, fail-closed
22.summary: Test suite validating behavioral contracts from COMPATIBILITY.md and OPERATIONAL_LIMITS.md (PR-4). Python tests: unknown message type handling (MessageEnvelope with unrecognized type logs warning, does not crash), oversized payload rejection (>16MB raises ValidationError), unknown field preservation (extra fields survive round-trip), retry behavior simulation (mock transport with configurable failure, assert exponential backoff with jitter and max 3 retries), fail-closed verification (missing required security fields cause rejection, not default-fill). Swift tests mirror unknown message and size limit tests.

23.title: PRD-001 completion checklist, contract freeze certification, and final REPO_LAYOUT update
23.summary: Final documentation PR certifying all PRD-001 deliverables are complete. forge-standards/CONTRACT_FREEZE.md documents frozen contract versions and the change process (schema changes require DEC-NNN entry and version bump). docs/decisions/PRD-001-COMPLETION.md has checklist of all functional requirements mapped to implementing PRs. Updated forge-standards/REPO_LAYOUT.md reflects final directory state. This is the formal gate: subsequent PRDs may depend on PRD-001 contracts only after this PR merges.
